column3: [{"role": "user", "metadata": {"title": null}, "content": "what is an fir ?"}, {"role": "assistant", "metadata": {"title": null}, "content": "- A First Information Report (FIR) is a formal complaint submitted to the police, initiating the investigation of a cognizable offense. - The purpose of filing an FIR is to provide the police with immediate information about a crime, allowing them to start their investigation promptly. - The process of filing an FIR involves providing a statement of the incident to designated police authorities, who then register the complaint and assign a unique number for tracking purposes. - Once an FIR is filed, the police have the authority to conduct investigations, gather evidence, and take appropriate actions based on the findings.."}, {"role": "user", "metadata": {"title": null}, "content": "how to file it "}, {"role": "assistant", "metadata": {"title": null}, "content": "- How to file taxes: gather necessary documents, calculate tax liability, fill out tax forms, submit electronically or via mail. - Documents provided focus on court cases and legal procedures, not tax filing instructions. - IRS website offers comprehensive tax filing information, including forms and instructions. - Consult a tax professional or visit the IRS website for personalized guidance and assistance.."}]
